name: Tour da Eira (PR 4) — circular route in Serra da Estrela
name_original: Rota da Eira (PR 4) — percurso circular na Serra da Estrela
region_intro: Family friendly hiking trails around Loriga are set within the dramatic glacial valley of the Serra da Estrela Natural Park in Portugal. This region is defined by its U-shaped glacial valleys, carved by ancient ice, featuring steep slopes, rocky outcrops, and high-altitude plateaus. The landscape includes granite boulders, cold streams, and waterfalls, with the highest point in mainland Portugal, Torre (1993 meters), located within the park. Best family friendly hiking trails around Loriga The most popular family friendly hiking route is Covão do Curral Dam – Covão do Ferro Lake loop from Serra da Estrela, a 3.2 miles (5.1 km) trail that takes 1 hour 34 minutes to complete. This route features a moderate elevation gain of 589 feet (180 meters). Another top favourite among local hikers is Small Cave – Serra da Estrela Waterfall loop from Loriga, an easy 1.4 miles (2.2 km) path. This trail offers views of waterfalls and is suitable for a shorter family outing. Local hikers also love the Torre (Serra da Estrela) – Torre Summit, Serra da Estrela loop from Serra da Estrela, a 5.6 miles (8.9 km) trail leading through the high-altitude terrain of Serra da Estrela, often completed in about 3 hours 24 minutes. Hiking around Loriga is defined by glacial valleys, granite landscapes, and high-altitude plateaus. The network offers options for different ability levels, from easy family walks to more challenging moderate routes. The routes in Loriga are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 4.36 stars from over 900 reviews. More than 3,600 hikers have used komoot to explore Loriga's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many family-friendly hiking trails are available around Loriga?
A: Komoot offers a selection of over 40 family-friendly hiking and walking routes around Loriga. These trails are designed to be enjoyable for all ages, with manageable distances and gentle paths.

Q: What is the best time of year for family hikes in Loriga?
A: The warmer summer months, from June to September, are ideal for hiking and enjoying the river beaches. Spring (May to early June) is also excellent, with lush green valleys and flowing waterfalls, while autumn (late September to October) offers crisp air and golden hills, perfect for comfortable walks.

Q: Are there any easy circular routes suitable for families with children?
A: Yes, there are several easy circular routes. For a shorter option, consider the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/32172467" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Small Cave – Serra da Estrela Waterfall loop from Loriga&lt;/a&gt;, which is just over 2 km long. The region is known for its accessible circular routes offering good views, often passing through terraced farmland.

Q: Can we find trails with waterfalls or natural swimming spots in Loriga?
A: Absolutely! The region boasts beautiful water features. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/32172467" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Small Cave – Serra da Estrela Waterfall loop from Loriga&lt;/a&gt; leads to a waterfall. For refreshing dips, Loriga offers crystal-clear river beaches like &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/974890" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Lapa dos Dinheiros River Beach&lt;/a&gt;, known for its inviting water and ancient chestnut trees, or the popular Loriga river beach.

Q: What kind of natural features and landmarks can we expect to see on family hikes?
A: Loriga, nestled in the Serra da Estrela Natural Park, is famous for its dramatic U-shaped glacial valleys, rocky outcrops, and high-altitude plateaus. You might encounter ancient chestnut trees, granite blocks, and in spring, vibrant wildflowers. Notable landmarks include the fascinating &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/374228" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Covão dos Conchos Trail&lt;/a&gt;, leading to a unique gaping hole in a remote mountain lake, and the serene &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/4915608" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Long Lake&lt;/a&gt;.

Q: Are there any trails suitable for toddlers or with stroller access?
A: While many trails are gentle, truly stroller-accessible trails are less common due to the natural, sometimes rugged terrain of the Serra da Estrela. However, many of the 'easy' rated routes, like the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/32172467" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Small Cave – Serra da Estrela Waterfall loop&lt;/a&gt;, are relatively short and have less challenging surfaces, making them more manageable for families with toddlers, though a sturdy off-road stroller or carrier is recommended.

Q: What do other hikers enjoy most about hiking in Loriga?
A: The area is highly rated by the komoot community, with an average score of 4.36 stars. Reviewers often praise the stunning glacial landscapes, the tranquility of the trails, and the opportunity to discover hidden waterfalls and river beaches. The unique blend of natural beauty and historical significance also receives high marks.

Q: Are there any dog-friendly trails in the Loriga area?
A: Many trails within the Serra da Estrela Natural Park are dog-friendly, allowing you to bring your furry companions along. It's always advisable to keep dogs on a leash, especially in areas with livestock or protected wildlife, and to carry waste bags. Check specific trail regulations if you plan to visit very sensitive areas.

Q: Where can we find parking at the trailheads for family hikes?
A: Parking availability varies by trailhead. For routes starting directly from Loriga village, you can often find parking within the village itself. For trails further afield, like those near the dams or higher up in the Serra da Estrela, designated parking areas are usually available. It's recommended to check the specific route details on komoot for parking information for your chosen trail.

Q: Are there any longer, but still family-friendly, routes that offer more extensive views?
A: For families looking for a slightly longer adventure with expansive views, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/10370014" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Covão do Curral Dam – Covão do Ferro Lake loop from Serra da Estrela&lt;/a&gt; is an excellent choice. This easy route covers just over 5 km and offers beautiful scenery around the glacial lakes and dams, providing a great sense of the region's unique landscape without being overly strenuous.
tips: A beautiful ancient path that has not yet been discovered by tourism.

Among the terraces, chestnut groves and pine forests, a rural heritage that is as authentic as it is surprising is hidden. Like this house, for example, which seems to have grown out of the rock itself: embedded in a huge boulder, in a perfect symbiosis between popular architecture and raw nature. It is as if it had been sculpted by the mountain.

waypoints.name: Stone House of Serra da Estrela
